que: What is the primary purpose of the Flexible widget in Flutter?
options: To control how a child of a Row, Column, or Flex flexes
To add padding around a child
To align a child to the bottom

que: Compared to Expanded, Flexible allows a child to do what?
options: Occupy available space with more control

que: In which parent widgets can Flexible be used?
According to the constructor, what is the default value of the flex parameter in Flexible?
What is the default value of the fit parameter in Flexible?
Which parameter of Flexible is required?
What does the flex parameter in Flexible represent?
options: The flex factor determining how much space this child takes relative to others

que: What does the fit parameter in Flexible control?
options: How the child should fit within the available space (tight or loose)

que: In the example Row, what are the flex values of the two Flexible widgets?
In the example Row, what colors are used for the two Container widgets inside Flexible?
If one Flexible has flex 2 and another has flex 1 in the same Row, how is horizontal space divided?
options: First gets one-third, second gets two-thirds
First gets two-thirds, second gets one-third

que: According to the quick tips, when should you use Flexible?
options: For widgets that should always have a fixed size
For widgets that should take up available space but not necessarily all of it

que: Which widget is suggested to combine with Flexible for complex layouts?
Which is listed as something to avoid with Flexible?
options: Using Flexible outside of Row, Column, or Flex

que: What is another thing to avoid with Flexible according to the file?
options: Using Flexible when a fixed size is needed
Using it with only one child

que: Which FlexFit value makes the child take up all the available space?
Which FlexFit value allows the child to be at most as large as the available space but not forced to fill it?
How does Flexible differ conceptually from Expanded?
options: Flexible ignores flex, Expanded uses it
Flexible allows loose or tight fit, Expanded is effectively always tight
Flexible can only be used in Column, Expanded only in Row
Flexible is only for vertical layouts

que: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a valid parent for Flexible?
Which statement best summarizes the Flexible widget?
options: A widget that centers its child within its parent
A widget that controls how a child of Row, Column, or Flex uses available space via flex and fit, offering more control than Expanded
A widget that scrolls its child
A widget that overlays children on top of each other
